user.title: Devices
user.description: Select the climate device to control and the indoor temperature sensor.

data_description.target_entity: The climate entity that will be commanded (e.g. your AC or heat pump).
data_description.temp_sensor: Temperature sensor used to display the current indoor temperature.

schedule.title: Schedules
schedule.description: Choose the schedule helpers that define when Comfort and Eco modes are active. They must be different entities.

data_description.comfort_schedule: When this schedule is on, the system targets comfort setpoints.
data_description.eco_schedule: When this schedule is on (and comfort is off), the system targets eco setpoints.

presence.title: Presence
presence.description: Optionally select presence sensors so the system can switch to Eco when nobody is home and pre-condition before you return.

data_description.presence_sensors: One or more binary sensors (presence, occupancy, or motion). Leave empty to disable presence detection.
data_description.precondition_min: Minutes before your expected return when the system starts heating or cooling to comfort setpoints.

settings.title: Setpoints & Updates
settings.description: Set temperature targets for each mode. Optionally connect a solar inverter or weather entity to enable solar-aware pre-cooling.

data_description.comfort_heat: Target heating temperature when someone is home.
data_description.comfort_cool: Target cooling temperature when someone is home.
data_description.eco_heat: Target heating temperature when nobody is home. Must be lower than the comfort heating setpoint.
data_description.eco_cool: Target cooling temperature when nobody is home. Must be higher than the comfort cooling setpoint.
data_description.solar_power_sensor: Inverter output sensor (W or kW). When solar production is high, the cooling setpoint is lowered to use free energy.
data_description.weather_entity: Used to look ahead for sunny conditions and pre-cool before solar production peaks.
data_description.update_interval: How often the coordinator recalculates and pushes new setpoints to the device.

error.same_entity: Comfort and Eco schedules must be different entities.
error.comfort_heat_gte_cool: Comfort heating setpoint must be lower than the comfort cooling setpoint.
error.eco_heat_gt_comfort: Eco heating setpoint must not be higher than the comfort heating setpoint.
error.eco_cool_lt_comfort: Eco cooling setpoint must not be lower than the comfort cooling setpoint.

init.title: Climate Control Settings
init.description: Update schedules, presence sensors, setpoints, and solar settings.

data_description.comfort_schedule: When this schedule is on, the system targets comfort setpoints.
data_description.eco_schedule: When this schedule is on (and comfort is off), the system targets eco setpoints.
data_description.presence_sensors: One or more binary sensors. Leave empty to disable presence detection.
data_description.precondition_min: Minutes before your expected return when pre-conditioning starts.
data_description.comfort_heat: Target heating temperature when someone is home.
data_description.comfort_cool: Target cooling temperature when someone is home.
data_description.eco_heat: Target heating temperature when nobody is home.
data_description.eco_cool: Target cooling temperature when nobody is home.
data_description.solar_power_sensor: Inverter output sensor (W or kW) for solar-aware pre-cooling.
data_description.weather_entity: Used to look ahead for sunny conditions.
data_description.update_interval: How often the coordinator recalculates and pushes new setpoints.

error.same_entity: Comfort and Eco schedules must be different entities.
error.comfort_heat_gte_cool: Comfort heating setpoint must be lower than the comfort cooling setpoint.
error.eco_heat_gt_comfort: Eco heating setpoint must not be higher than the comfort heating setpoint.
error.eco_cool_lt_comfort: Eco cooling setpoint must not be lower than the comfort cooling setpoint.
